description: Lock Water-Powered Blast Furnaces to blast-furnace installations powered by water-wheel-driven bellows, separate from water-powered trip-hammer forge machinery.

checks.reason: The Lapphyttan evidence is a blast-furnace installation, so the furnace architecture remains a direct prerequisite.
The water-powered scope requires dam and water-wheel machinery to power the bellows.
Water-wheel machinery enables the powered bellows without making generic watermills the whole furnace technology.
The source-backed reconstruction uses leather bellows to keep the furnace hot and provide the forced-air blast.
Water-powered trip hammers are separate hammer/forge machinery rather than the mechanism that powers blast-furnace bellows.
The node remains anchored to late-twelfth-century Lapphyttan/Bergslagen evidence at century precision.
